Now my family, let me just tell you about some beliefs that I grew up with. We had this guy who was called the Philip Rushman. Now I don't know if you have them, you know, wherever you live, but this is a door-to-door hairbrush salesman. And when he would knock on my door ring to buzzer, my father would be like, you know, who is it? And he'd say, it's the Philip Rushman. And I'd be like, Jesus, age-facts. You know, who got that? And he wanted to have the guy thrown in jail, call the police, right? My parents hated salespeople. They hated them. You know, how proud they are of me right now, right? But anyway, we had the curvy vacuum guy, right? You know, my father, get him out of my house, right? You know, I was told as a kid, and maybe you were told this too, don't trust the salesman, you know, don't take candy from strangers, don't trust anyone. They're all out to get you. The whole world's out to get you. You know, I grew up like that, being spoon-fed, those beliefs from both my mom and my dad. And guess what? I rejected them. See, typically what we do is either one of two things. We will model our parents exactly or we'll go the exact other way. Well, in my case, obviously I went the exact opposite way. Typically it's not the middle ground. So I rejected all that stuff, and now I'm essentially your dream client. You don't have to get me to a 10, 10, 10. You get me to a 7, 6, 7. I'm like, okay, I'll give it a whirl. What the hell, right? Because the belief I have says, smart decisions are made quickly, not painfully slowly. And I'm going to trust someone that, you know, okay, I'm not the suspicious of people. I'll go with my gut and bam, sales, I want to make a commission. Nothing wrong with that. You know, I'm smart enough myself to make an informed decision. No one's going to take advantage of me. You know, that's just my beliefs, not right or wrong, they're my beliefs. And what happens is that people of a high action threshold are loaded with the opposite beliefs, these limiting beliefs about buying. So what we learn to do here is we lower their action threshold during the sale. Now one way to do that are very simple ways by offering them a guarantee. You know, you guarantee your product, you're good at your service. The second way is by offering them a cooling off period where they can actually make the decision now but they can still reverse it for three or four days, right? That's another second way. But the most powerful way, and I'm going to talk about this in much greater detail on the looping module, the more powerful way is we actually make them run the movie again. Except this time we make sure it's a different movie. So we give them reasons why they'll feel massive amounts of pleasure and very little pain if they make the decision. And if they don't make the decision why they'll feel no pleasure at all and massive amounts of pain. Essentially we actually lay out that scenario I just mentioned. What's the worst that can possibly happen? We make them run the movie logically except on our terms. An example would be this. I'd say to the client who's sitting on the fence, let's say I'm wrong. Let's say the stock I try to sell you is going south and it goes down two points. You got to stop Wilson Place and you lose $4,000. Is that going to put you in the poor house? Is that going to make you a poor man? And they'll say, well, no, of course not. You say exactly. And on the upside, let's say I'm right and the stock goes up 10 points. So you make, you know, five, 10, $15,000. It's not going to make you the richest man on earth but it will do is serve as a benchmark for future business. Show you that I'm a guy that can not only put you into the market at the right time but also take you out. And what I could do for you over the long term in the way of new issues, aftermarket, market convergence, I could be an asset to you and your family and blah, blah, blah. I don't want to go through the whole thing. I do this in the looping module. But here's the point. When you run that scenario, that action threshold lowering scenario and you make them run a different movie, if you had them close, if they were actually 8, 9, 9 and in that moment you drop down their action threshold by making them run that better movie, bam, magic starts to happen. If these really, really tough non-buyers who could use your product, who actually need your product, you turn them into A, buyers in the moment and then once you have those people they become the best clients of all. People who have high action thresholds, they are great long term clients. They don't mind paying top dollar. They're great tippers. They're so happy. They finally found someone they can trust and connect with, a company that will stand behind them, a product they can enjoy and trust. Once you have that person, my dad just so you know, he's been doing the same dry cleaner, the same Chinese laundry, the same mechanic, the same everything for the last 45 years. Someone in whatever it was was able to lower his action threshold in that moment, got him as a client and he never, ever left. And that truly is the beauty of the straight line. Remember what I said at the beginning about closing everyone who's closable? This is what I was talking about. You see, these are the clients, these high action threshold people, the ones that typically slip away from most sales people. Once you understand the dynamics, the power of the straight line, these people become literally laid down to you and you get to that point where I'm at and where all the people I've trained are at, which is closing anyone who's closable.
